---
title: "AI Traceability"
subtitle: '"Safely Scaling" Human-AI Collaboration w/ Atlassian...'
author: "Justin Townsend"
profile: "[LNKD - JT](https://www.linkedin.com/in/justin-j-townsend/)"
atl_comm: "[ATL - JT](https://community.atlassian.com/user/profile/a248bc14-7d4d-48e9-8c30-0263f13bb552)"
description: |
  "Safely Scaling Human-AI Collaboration with Atlassian Intelligence using Rovo, 22nd April 2026."
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          <style>
          .callout.callout-style-simple {
            text-align: left;
            display: inline-flexbox;
          }
          </style>
    footnotes-hover: true
    reference-location: document
    footer: "[Justin Townsend](https://www.linkedin.com/in/justin-j-townsend/)"
    logo: /images/fat-tails-alt.png
    show-progress: true
    transition: fade
    transition-speed: default
    incremental: false
    lightbox:
      match: auto
    mermaid:
      theme: neutral
revealjs-plugins:
  - RevealjsTyped
filters:
  - roughnotation
---

## Context is Key 😀

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple" title="AI Traceability in Atlassian's Eco-System @ 22nd April 2026"}

* "Rovo" is the "umbrella" term for all of Atlassian's AI (user) features, including:
	+ Rovo Chat
	+ Studio (for Rovo)
  + Rovo Chrome extension
  + Rovo Dev (Chat)
  + Rovo Dev (CLI)
  + Rovo Dev (for Jira)
  + newly, Remix w/ Rovo AND...
* likely more to come!
  + New genAI tool combinations often increase LLM usage, through new model introduction and further token consumption.
:::

::: {.notes}
* Context is Key, as the "press" constantly reminds us just now...
* Some terminology may be less obvious to infrequent Atlassian users, so a brief explanation of the state-of-play today...
* I've dated the statement for reasons I'll mention in the upcoming disclaimer...
:::

## Thank You...
* [Atlasssian Community, London](https://ace.atlassian.com/london/), (Stuart, Jay, Bruce, Abi)
* Jesus F. and Lloyds for sharing their migration journey

::: {.notes}

::: {style="font-size:18px"}

* ACE, London: I hope I have your names correctly listed by length of service to ACE London
* Jesus Fernandez: Thank you, TOUGH act to follow.
  + Knowing more about the REAL decisions you're facing, and
  + Staring into the bank's crystal ball for enduring funding decisions... MUCH APPRECIATED
* No sooner do companies finish an epic migration to cloud (pun intended) do you turn to decisions about AI AND
  + Which ones to switch on...?

:::

:::

## {#slide4-id data-menu-title="Disclaimer"}
::: {.callout-note appearance="simple" title="Disclaimer @ 22nd April 2026"}
Views and opinions expressed in this presentation are solely my own and do not necessarily reflect the official positions, strategies, or views of any organisation I represent. While every effort has been made to ensure that principles presented here are well-considered to move forward constructively with embedded A.I., they may be superseded or invalidated by features, changes, or developments introduced in future Atlassian releases.
:::

::: {.notes}

::: {style="font-size:18px"}

* AI is a HUGE topic and embedded AI is just one aspect of it. I've certainly been "conflicted" about what to cover this evening
* What you'll see shouldn't be considered "best practice" or a "how-to" guide, it is hoped it'll motivate your thinking on what's currently possible for scaled AI (with Rovo)
  + AND what might need to come, to satisfy auditability / intervention
* I've tried to cram a bunch of things in, so if something is unclear as we go, put your hand up, I'll do my best to clarify / answer your questions...
* My details are at the end so we can get to the interesting stuff straightaway...

:::

:::

# {#slide5-id data-menu-title="Jira's Many Faces..."}

![](/images/jira-256px.png "Jira"){.nostretch fig-align="center"}

::: {.notes}
* Jira is arguably Atlassian's most recognised product
* To get participation going a bit
* Given the focus is on Atlassian products tonight, I thought
  + Let's get a Show of Hands for which of these flavours of Jira you recognize
* Each one is a screenshot (except one, which is text - itself a HINT)
  + HINT: I find focusing on the iconography and text on the screen can help date the instance type.
:::

## {#slide6-id data-menu-title="Jira's Faces... (1)"}

![](/images/jira-1.png){width=50% fig-align="center" .lightbox}

::: {.fragment style="text-align: left;"}
* Jira Data Center: Sprint Report
:::

::: {.notes}
* PLEASE SHOUTOUT IF TOO SMALL
* Hands-up if you recognise this as Jira?
  + Keep your hands-up if you think you recognise which flavour of Jira this is?
	+ Anyone willing to shout out which one?
* Sprint Report, Jira Data Center
:::

## {#slide7-id data-menu-title="Jira's Faces... (2)"}

![](/images/jira-2.png){width=50% fig-align="center" .lightbox}

::: {.fragment style="text-align: left;"}
* Jira Cloud: Spaces (Project) Board
:::

::: {.notes}
* Again, PLEASE SHOUTOUT IF TOO SMALL
* Again, hands-up if you recognise this as Jira?
* Keep your hands-up if you think you recognise which flavour of Jira this is?
* Anyone willing to shout out which one?
* Project Board, Jira Cloud
:::

## {#slide8-id data-menu-title="Jira's Faces... (3)"}

:::{ .typed data-typed-speed="40" data-typed-reset-on-slide-change="true" data-typed-start-delay="1000" data-typed-show-cursor="false" }
git commit -m 'FT-12 #time 4h 30m #comment UI Updated.'
:::

::: {.fragment style="text-align: left;"}
* hashtags might give it away...
	+ [#time...]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=red},
	+ [#comment...]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=red}
:::

::: {.fragment style="text-align: left;"}
* Jira Smart Commits: (Extended) Git CLI
:::

::: {.notes}

::: {style="font-size:18px"}

* Hands-up if you recognise this as Jira?
  + Keep your hands-up if you think you recognise which flavour of Jira this is?
	+ Anyone willing to shout out which one it is?
* Jira Smart Commits: (Extended) Git CLI
  + I remember doing this integration for a large telco (back in the day) thinking it was THE UNLOCK to productivity and transparency...
  + I was SOOO enthusiastic about it
    - Probably had some PowerPoint slides about it...
  + I think if you have a role in "DevRel", "hats-off" to you...
    - it's sometimes hard to know what engineers / developers may care about

:::

:::

## {#slide8-id data-menu-title="Jira's Faces... (4)" visibility="hidden"}

![](/images/jira-4.png){.lightbox width=50% fig-align="center"}

::: {.fragment style="text-align: left;"}
* VS Code - Atlassian extension: + Custom (JQL) filter
:::

::: {.notes}
* Again, PLEASE SHOUTOUT IF IT TOO SMALL
* Again, hands-up if you recognise this as Jira?
  + Keep your hands-up if you think you recognise which flavour of Jira this is?
	+ Anyone willing to shout out which one it is?
* Custom filter, Atlassian extension, VS Code
:::

## {#slide10-id data-menu-title="Jira's Faces... (5)"}

BUT, DO YOU Know Jira's Face like this...?

![](/images/jira-5.png){.lightbox width=75% fig-align="center"}

:::{.fragment }
* Modern, integrated Atlassian at YOUR point of interaction.
:::

::: {.notes}

::: {style="font-size:18px"}

* BUSY... Huh!
  + This is my IDE (VS Code here today, but I've had success with the setup in Cursor also)
	+ My agent Co-Pilot on the right side-bar (GitHub's Copilot, no proxy)
* Middle-Top the editor panel has a Jira ticket
  + Atlassian extension installed from the marketplace
    - the (*.vsix) extension might require internal approval
	+ I've configured it with a custom (JQL) filter to show me the work items I'm interested in, from the FT project (Jira space)
* Top-left, I've got a code repository (SVN connected, this one is Git, could be BitBucket)
* Middle-left, I've a collapsed panel with the (branch) source-control history
* Bottom-left, most relevant this evening, I've configured the new Rovo Dev extension
  + For tonight's presentation I've worked with CHAT
  + It's a bit more obvious what's happening
    - the CLI is also available
* On closer with the chat you can witness we're BOTH getting a bit confused
  + IM(H)O - companies already pay for "confusion / learning" of their employees
    - (in delays, extensions, re-basing timelines, learning, mentoring etc.),
	+ Perhaps AI chat can clear-up confusion more quickly
* Eagle-eyed amongst you SHOULD notice the open ticket (Top-Middle) and the Forge work taking place (Bottom-Middle) are for differnt Jira projects..
  + With a highly configurable IDE interface and Rovo Dev
	+ IT's REALISTIC to be working MORE consistently, on multiple projects at once!
* But CAN we get there SAFELY...?

:::

:::

# {#slide11-id data-menu-title="WHAT... is the point of all this?"}

![](/images/confused-man.png){.nostretch width=30% fig-align="center" .nolightbox}

WHAT... is the point of all this?

:::{.notes}
* Ways of interacting with Atlassian are increasing...
  + so work should be made easier?
  + it should feel more weightless
* For users to feel these benefits... 
  + We need some safety, some governance, BUT
  + To do this well
  + It should feel "close-to" invisible in the user experience
:::

## ✨ Weightless Work with Rovo?{.smaller}

:::{.fragment style="text-align: left;"}

* We're switching on the (embedded) AI
  + Let's avoid the strategic roadmap becoming our competitor's backlog
* Mitigate those [unintended consequences...]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=orange}

:::

:::{.fragment}

![](/images/embedded-ai-switchon.png)

:::

::: {.notes} 

::: {style="font-size:18px"}

* Switching it on, you're assuming responsibility (incl. Biases of integrated LLMs)
* We’re at a moment when genAI has entered every C-Suite discussion, but still at the edge of REAL impact for most businesses
* As a Programme Manager / Enterprise Architect, let's throw-up a framework to give some context here, I find ACE is pretty strasightforward
  + Align - Establishing boundaries for Rovo
  + Control - Controlling Rovo's domain for action (Prompt Design, Tool Permissions, Available Skills)
  + Evidence - Intervention States, Session Analytics, Drift Analysis

:::

:::

## {#slide13-id data-menu-title="How Rovo Helps Us Right Now...?" .smaller}

|                    | Control                   | Evidence                        | Intervene |
|--------------------|:-------------------------:|:--------------------------------|:---------:|
| Admin              | ✅ Rovo access            | ✅ Rovo Insights               | ❌       |
| Audit              | -                         | ✅ ALQL = 'activity ~"Rovo"'   | -        |
| Insights           | -                         | ✅ Analytics                   | ❌       |
| Studio             | ✅ Rovo Agent Permissions | ❌                             | ❌      |
| Forge              | -                         | ✅ Developer Console           | ✅ / ❌ |
| Extension (*.vsix) | ✅ Rovo Dev Settings     | ✅ Logs                        | ✅ / ❌ | 

: Rovo Control & Insights (TOGAF View)

:::{.notes}
* So if we TOGAF out a "Rovo View"
  + What is Rovo doing to support here?
* Table requires a little explanation.
  + With Rovo it turns out we can ALREADY DO, quite a lot
  + Turns out, quite alot
  + From the Admin console we can control Rovo's access
  + We can see what Rovo's been up to, with the Insights
  + From Audit Logs and Insights 
:::

## Rovo (Dev) -> Tell Me MORE?{.smaller}

:::{.fragment style="text-align: left;"}
* Where THE WORK starts
  + What are ALL those Rovos doing?
:::

:::{.fragment}

::: {#fig-rovoforge layout-ncol=2}
![Rovo Dev (Sonnet 4.5) -> Forge App, GraphQL API](/images/rovo-atlassian-external.png){.lightbox group="rovo-forge"}

![Forge App -> Missing Scope](/images/rovo-atlassian-external-error.png){.lightbox group="rovo-forge"}

Rovo Analysis with a Forge App?
:::

:::

:::{.fragment style="text-align: left;"}
* ❌ OK, another approach?
:::

::: {.notes}
* Rovo Dev has a 'YOLO Mode', it looks a bit MUCH for us just now
  + Even so, with Rovo Dev (set to Sonnet 4.5)
  + I've vibed-out a Forge app
    - Designed to dig deeper into all those Rovos
	+ All seems to be going well, THEN... UH OH!
:::

## {#slide15-id data-menu-title="Rovo (Dev) -> Forge Architecture" .smaller}

![AI Traceability -> Forge App Architecture](/images/rovo-forgeapp.svg){.nolightbox}

:::{.fragment style="text-align: left;"}
* So where next?
  + EVEN more Rovo Dev features are available
    - Sessions, Logging etc.
  + I have renewed "hope"... 
* Vibe-out more features for my Forge App
  + Can I load some Rovo Dev session logs to prove a point? [^1]
:::

[^1]: Solution shown is sub-optimal at-scale, consider a dedicated SRE tool.

::: {.notes}
* At this point an overview of the Forge App solutions architecture is warranted...
  1. User Interface (Atlassian's UI Kit)
  2. Forge Remote Backend (another Forge App)
    2.1 Used to reach the GraphQL API
  3. Local storage to Forge (KVS)
  4. GraphQL API (OAuth)
  5. Resolvers link frontend and backend components together
    5.1 In this example, "Sync Agents from GraphQL API" button in the app
:::

## Rovo (Dev) -> More to Give? {.smaller}

![Rovo Dev -> Limit Exceeded](/images/rovodev-credit-limit-exceeded.png){.lightbox width=50% fig-align="center"}

:::{.fragment style="text-align: left;"}
* After 8.5 days of the month, I bust through the orgnisation's token pool [^2]
  + NO MORE for anyone else it seems
  + Back to the budget-holder cap-in-hand?
* BUT is this MY fault?
  + NO separate limits for development and testing, as yet...?
:::

[^2]: Some AI solutions make token consumption a 1st class citizen.

::: {.notes}
* In a small business, where the budget holder is the product developer
  + ABSOLUTELY, this responsibility sits with one person
* In a larger business, this would be devolved...
  + and the unit cost efficiency of Rovo credits is the shared responsibility of Finance / IT mgt
  + In a sophisticated large enterprise, a specialist AI / ML / Model Risk function would give valuable input
* Resource sharing is something to be learned, across teams, over time.
  + Some similarities to resource pooling in Spark, or
  + pod resource management in Kubernetes
:::

## Rovo (Dev) -> "Sense Making" {.smaller}

[![](/images/rovo-session-analytics-d3radial.png){width=75%}](embedded-ai-rovo-d3-radial.html)

* No sub-agents this time, BUT
* +220 LLM calls, +200 tool calls later...

::: {.notes}

::: {style="font-size:18px"}
* Radial chart layouts can be intuitive for "getting-to-grips" with what's going on in an agent session.
  + In our example it took several sessions to get the Forge App from earlier, BUT
  + For illustration purposes, I've added a single Rovo Dev session log here...
* If you'll let me explain
* D3's radial tree, is a circular tree-layout plot...
  + the central point provides the user / agent perspective
  + the start of the chart (it's first messages) appear just to the right of 12 o'clock
  + subsequent messages continue round in a clockwise manner
* Edges are coloured BLUE for a request (from me, or a tool), RED for a response
  + Nodes are coloured coded by type (system / user-prompt, text, tool-call etc.)
    - calls to the LLM (in this case Anthropic) are FURTHER identified with a GREY circle surrounding the node
* We can explore what's happened, by hovering over the edges and nodes of the chart

:::

:::

## Rovo -> Where Does That Leave US?{.smaller}

* Control and guidance ✅ ([Settings](https://support.atlassian.com/rovo/docs/manage-rovo-dev-cli-settings/), [AGENTS.md](https://support.atlassian.com/rovo/docs/use-memory-in-rovo-dev-cli/), [MCP Configuration](https://support.atlassian.com/rovo/docs/rovo-dev-and-model-context-protocol-mcp/))
* Reports and tracing ✅ ([Insights](https://www.atlassian.com/software/rovo/guides/admin-guide/monitoring-dashboards#access-rovo-insights), [Session Logs](https://support.atlassian.com/rovo/docs/manage-sessions-in-rovo-dev-cli/#Tips-for-working-with-sessions)) [^3] 
BUT
* Runtime intervention ❌, needs change / customisation  
* In the meantime?
  + DPA caution
  + "Sandbox" my Rovo prompts before deployment?
    - Monitor emergent behaviour in logs
  + Re-cycle the sandbox for new prompts
    - Periodically deliver new "prompts" designed to guide agent behaviour...

[^3]: [CDAC-99756: rovo:atlassian-external](https://community.developer.atlassian.com/t/forge-storage-forge-remote-graphql-rovo-atlassian-external/99756)

:::{.notes}
* I've got control, I've got guidance
* I've got reports, I've got tracing
* BUT we're not quite there with intervention...
  + SO... be cautious with Atlassian's [current](https://support.atlassian.com/rovo/docs/rovo-data-privacy-and-usage-guidelines/) and [evolving](https://support.atlassian.com/security-and-access-policies/docs/manage-data-contribution-for-your-atlassian-organization/) DPAs and
  + Current impact for your business (an amount of extra-territorial processing)
* (SOME) of the capability is there, BUT the accountability is yours
:::

## 🔮 Rovo (Dev) -> Near Future...?{.smaller}

:::{.fragment style="text-align: left;"}

* Leading agent engineering platforms [provide a hint]{.rn-fragment rn-type="highlight" rn-color="yellow"}?

:::

:::{.fragment}

```{.bash code-line-numbers="1,3,8"}
acli rovodev run /hooks

acli rovodev run /hooks before_agent
acli rovodev run /hooks after_agent
acli rovodev run /hooks before_model
acli rovodev run /hooks after_model

acli rovodev run /hooks wrap_model_call
acli rovodev run /hooks wrap_tool_call
```

:::

:::{.fragment}
  
🤷🏻‍♂️  🤷🏻‍♂️  🤷🏻‍♂️  🤷🏻‍♂️  🤷🏻‍♂️ ?
  
:::

:::{.notes}
* Look to leading AI tools for guidance?
* Maybe some hooks for Rovo, to enable "intervention" approaches
:::

# Why Listen to Me...?{footer=false}

![](/images/jt-profile.png){.nolightbox width=75% fig-align="center"}
